text: Mobile app development involves creating software for smartphones and tablets. A key principle is responsive design, which ensures the app looks and works well on different screen sizes. Developers must choose between native development, where apps are built specifically for iOS or Android, and cross-platform development, which uses a single codebase for both systems. Once completed, the app must be submitted to app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store for approval. Performance is also critical; users expect fast load times and smooth navigation. A slow or buggy app can quickly lose users, so testing is essential before launch.
